name: AI Learning Machine v2 - Autonomous Research Engine (Enhanced)

parameters.formDescription: Enter a topic and the Autonomous Research Engine will decompose it into sub-questions, pull YouTube transcripts, scrape real web articles, and generate a comprehensive cited learning document â€” delivered to your Gmail and Telegram instantly.

values.placeholder: e.g., AI Agents and Multi-Agent Frameworks
Specific questions or areas you want the research to focus on

nodes.name: ðŸ”§ What Do You Want to Learn?

messages.content: You are a research planning AI. Your job is to decompose a learning topic into 5-8 focused sub-questions that together provide complete, non-overlapping coverage. Also generate optimized search queries for YouTube and web research. You MUST return ONLY valid JSON â€” no markdown, no code blocks, no explanation text.

={{ 'Topic: ' + $json.topic + '
Learning Level: ' + $json.level + '
Specific Focus: ' + ($json.specificQuestions || 'Cover all major aspects') + '

Decompose this topic into 5-8 sub-questions and generate search queries.

Return ONLY this JSON format:
{
  "sub_questions": ["What is X?", "How does X work?", "..."],
  "youtube_queries": ["X explained tutorial", "X how it works 2026", "..."],
  "web_queries": ["X complete guide 2026", "X best practices", "X explained"]
}

Rules:
- Sub-questions: 5-8, progressing from foundational to advanced
- YouTube queries: exactly 5, optimized for educational video content
- Web queries: exactly 3, optimized for in-depth articles and guides
- Return ONLY the JSON object' }}

={{ 'You are a world-class research assistant and educator. ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.levelInstruction + ' You MUST include inline citations in the format [Source: Title - URL] for every factual claim that comes from the research material. Return well-structured content with clear sections, explanations, examples, and key takeaways. Use markdown formatting.' }}

={{ 'Create a comprehensive, research-grounded learning document on: ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.topic + '

Student level: ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.level + '
Specific questions: ' + ($('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.specificQuestions || 'Cover all major aspects') + '

=== RESEARCH MATERIAL (use this as your primary source â€” cite every claim) ===

' + $('ðŸ§© Build RAG Context').first().json.ragContext + '

=== DOCUMENT STRUCTURE (follow this exactly) ===

## 1. What Is [Topic]? (Clear definition and why it matters)
## 2. How Does It Actually Work? (Mechanics explained with evidence from sources)
## 3. Key Concepts You Must Understand (5-10 core concepts with citations)
## 4. Real-World Applications and Examples (At least 5 practical examples)
## 5. Step-by-Step: How to Get Started (Actionable first steps)
## 6. Common Tools and Platforms (What exists, which are free, which are best)
## 7. Common Mistakes and Misconceptions (What beginners get wrong)
## 8. Advanced Topics for Further Learning (What to explore next)
## 9. Key Questions to Test Your Understanding (10 self-assessment questions)
## 10. Recommended Resources (Videos and articles from the research above)

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Write 2500-3000 words
- Include [Source: Title - URL] citation for EVERY claim from research material
- Be thorough, practical, and engaging
- Use specific numbers, statistics, and examples
- Reference the YouTube videos and web sources provided above' }}

={{ 'You are a world-class research assistant and subject matter expert. ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.levelInstruction + ' You MUST include inline citations in the format [Source: Title - URL] for every factual claim from the research material. Deliver the most thorough, analytically rigorous document possible. Use markdown formatting.' }}

={{ 'Create an expert-level, deeply researched learning document on: ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.topic + '

Expert level: ' + $('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.level + '
Specific focus: ' + ($('ðŸ”§ Parse Learning Request').first().json.specificQuestions || 'Maximum depth on all aspects') + '

=== RESEARCH MATERIAL (cite every claim using [Source: Title - URL]) ===

' + $('ðŸ§© Build RAG Context').first().json.ragContext + '

=== DOCUMENT STRUCTURE ===

## 1. What Is [Topic]? (Expert-level definition with historical context)
## 2. How Does It Actually Work? (Deep technical mechanics with evidence)
## 3. Key Concepts You Must Understand (10+ core concepts with nuance)
## 4. Real-World Applications and Case Studies (Detailed examples with data)
## 5. Implementation Guide (Advanced step-by-step with trade-offs)
## 6. Tools, Architectures, and Frameworks (Comparative analysis)
## 7. Common Pitfalls and Edge Cases (Expert-level gotchas)
## 8. Cutting-Edge Research and Future Directions (2024-2026 developments)
## 9. Critical Thinking Questions (15 advanced assessment questions)
## 10. Curated Resources (Videos, papers, and articles from research)

IMPORTANT:
- Write 2500-3500 words
- Include [Source: Title - URL] citations for ALL factual claims
- Go deeper than any standard guide
- Challenge conventional assumptions where evidence supports it
- Reference specific data points from the transcripts and web sources' }}
You are a document quality auditor. Score the given document excerpt on 5 criteria, each 1-10. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no explanation.

={{ 'Score this learning document on 5 criteria (1-10 each):

1. Depth - How thoroughly does it cover the topic?
2. Accuracy - Are claims supported with citations or evidence?
3. Clarity - Is it well-structured and easy to follow?
4. Sources - Does it reference diverse sources (videos, articles)?
5. Actionability - Does it provide practical next steps?

Document excerpt (first 2000 chars):
' + ($input.first().json?.message?.content || '').slice(0, 2000) + '

Return ONLY this JSON:
{"depth":N,"accuracy":N,"clarity":N,"sources":N,"actionability":N,"total":N}' }}

googleDocsOAuth2Api.name: AI Learning Machine - Google Docs API

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ requests: [{ insertText: { location: { index: 1 }, text: $('ðŸ“ Compile Master Document').first().json.masterContent } }] }) }}
